title: aciphylla montana var. gracilis
intro: Aciphylla montana var. gracilis, also known as Mountain Carrot or Kauri Fern, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It is traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant include treating colds, fever, and skin irritations, as well as acting as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Aciphylla montana var. gracilis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
